QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_5

question.text: What is the threshold for an RTA to be classified as a Qualified RTA (QRTA)?

answer.text: More than 5 crore folios under management
More than Rs.1 lakh crore AUM under management
More than 2 crore folios under management

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI classifies an RTA as a QRTA when it manages more than 2 crore (20 million) folios. QRTAs are considered Critical Infrastructure Institutions due to the volume of investor data and transactions they handle. **The Trap:** The threshold is folio-based, not AUM-based or client-based. Many candidates confuse the metric. It is purely about the number of investor folios. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers SEBI's oversight framework; QRTA classification brings enhanced regulatory requirements similar to systemically important financial institutions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both CAMS and KFintech qualify as QRTAs. When choosing platforms for your clients, know that QRTAs have stricter BCP and data protection requirements, providing an extra layer of safety.
